DOCUMENT 1 OF 5 – HOSPITAL DISCHARGE SUMMARY
HOSPITAL DISCHARGE SUMMARY
PATIENT & ADMISSION DETAILS
Patient Name: Mr. Ravi Kumar Sharma
Age / Gender: 47 Years / Male
Date of Birth: 14-March-1977
Address: Flat 4B, Green Valley Apts, Miyapur, Hyderabad - 500049, Telangana
Contact No.: +91-98491-23456
Aadhaar No.: XXXX-XXXX-7812 (masked)
Insurance Policy No. NSHP-HYD-2021-004471
Member ID: MEM-20210044711
TPA / Insurer: MediAssist TPA Pvt. Ltd. / New India Assurance Co. Ltd.
IP / MRN No.: IP-2024-08-00312
Ward / Bed: Ward C2 / Bed 14 (General Private)
ADMISSION & DISCHARGE
Date & Time of Admission: 12-August-2024 | 09:35 hrs
Date & Time of Discharge: 18-August-2024 | 13:00 hrs
Length of Stay: 6 days
Admitting Department: Cardiology / Internal Medicine
Treating Consultant: Dr. Meenakshi Raghavan (MBBS, MD – Cardiology, DM – Interventional Cardiology)
Registration No.: AP-MCI-2008-07412
CLINICAL INFORMATION
Chief Complaint:
Chest pain (substernal, radiating to left arm) with exertional dyspnea and diaphoresis for 6 hours prior to presentation.
History of Presenting Illness:
Mr. Ravi Kumar Sharma, a 47-year-old male with known hypertension (on amlodipine 5 mg) and Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (on
metformin 500 mg BD), presented to the Emergency Department of Apollo Prime Hospitals on 12-August-2024 at 09:35 hrs with
acute-onset chest pain of 6-hours duration, associated with left-arm radiation, diaphoresis, and mild breathlessness on exertion.
No syncope. No prior cardiac history. Smoker (10 pack-years, quit 3 years ago). Family history: father – MI at age 52.
Past Medical History:
1. Hypertension – 5 years 2. Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus – 3 years 3. Dyslipidaemia – diagnosed incidentally 4. No prior surgeries
or hospitalizations.
Examination Findings at Admission:
BP: 158/96 mmHg (right arm, sitting)
Pulse: 98 bpm, irregular
SpO2: 96% on room air
Temperature: 98.6 deg F (afebrile)
RR: 20 breaths/min
Weight / BMI: 84 kg / 29.1 kg/m2
CVS: S1 S2 heard; no murmurs; mild pedal edema bilateral
RS: Bilateral air entry equal; no added sounds
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender; no organomegaly
CNS: Conscious, alert, oriented to time, place, person

ocr_pages.raw_text:
INVESTIGATIONS SUMMARY
12-lead ECG on admission: ST-segment elevation in leads II, III, aVF (inferior STEMI pattern). Troponin-I (high-sensitivity): 4.82
ng/mL (elevated). 2D Echocardiography: EF 42%, inferior wall hypokinesia, mild MR Grade I. Complete blood picture, RFT, LFT,
lipid profile, HbA1c performed (refer Laboratory Report – Document 3 for full details).
PROCEDURES PERFORMED
# Procedure Date CPT Code Surgeon / Operator
1 Emergency Coronary Angiography (CAG) 12-Aug-2024 93454 Dr. Meenakshi Raghavan
2 PCI – Drug-Eluting Stent to RCA (proximal) 12-Aug-2024 92928 Dr. Meenakshi Raghavan
3 2D Echocardiography with Doppler 13-Aug-2024 93306 Dr. P. Srinivas (Radiology)
4 Continuous Cardiac Monitoring (CCU) 12-14 Aug 99354 Nursing / Cardiology Team
FINAL DIAGNOSIS
Type Diagnosis ICD-10 Code
Principal ST-Elevation Myocardial Infarction (STEMI) – Inferior Wall I21.19
Secondary Hypertension, Essential – Uncontrolled I10
Secondary Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus – Without Complications E11.9
Secondary Dyslipidaemia – Mixed Hyperlipidaemia E78.2
Procedural Presence of Coronary Angioplasty Implant (DES-RCA) Z95.5
HOSPITAL COURSE SUMMARY
Patient shifted to CCU immediately on admission. Emergency CAG revealed 100% occlusion of the proximal RCA with TIMI 0
flow. PCI with Drug-Eluting Stent (Xience Sierra 3.5x18 mm) performed with successful reperfusion (TIMI 3 flow
post-intervention). IV heparin infusion for 48 hours. Dual antiplatelet therapy initiated. Blood glucose monitored 6-hourly. BP
controlled with IV labetalol then oral agents. Rosuvastatin 40 mg started. Transferred to general ward on 15-Aug-2024.
Discharged stable on 18-August-2024.
DISCHARGE CONDITION & ADVICE
Condition at Discharge: Stable. Ambulating independently. No chest pain at rest.
Diet: Low-sodium, low-fat, diabetic diet.
Activity: Restricted to light indoor activity for 2 weeks. No strenuous exertion.
Follow-Up: OPD appointment: Dr. Meenakshi Raghavan on 25-August-2024 at 10:00 AM.
Cardiac Rehab: Enrolment in Phase-II cardiac rehabilitation recommended.
Emergency Advice: Return to ER immediately if chest pain, breathlessness, or palpitations recur.
DISCHARGE MEDICATIONS
# Drug Name & Strength Route Frequency Duration Instructions
1 Tab. Aspirin 75 mg Oral Once daily Lifelong After food
2 Tab. Ticagrelor 90 mg Oral Twice daily 12 months Do not crush
3 Tab. Rosuvastatin 40 mg Oral Once at HS Lifelong At bedtime
4 Tab. Metoprolol Succinate 25 mg Oral Once daily Lifelong Morning
5 Tab. Ramipril 5 mg Oral Once daily Lifelong Morning
6 Tab. Amlodipine 5 mg Oral Once daily Ongoing Morning
7 Tab. Metformin 500 mg Oral Twice daily Ongoing After food
8 Tab. Pantoprazole 40 mg Oral Once daily 3 months Empty stomach
Dr. Meenakshi Raghavan | MD, DM – Interventional Cardiology | Reg. No. AP-MCI-2008-07412
Apollo Prime Hospitals Pvt. Ltd., Hyderabad | Date: 18-August-2024
